sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 2 actions in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: DOWN, DOWN.
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 37, level 1.
Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current world model from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE WORLD MODEL.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Working world model carried from earlier turns:
- World model: - **Blocks**: Orange (2 rows) + blue (3 rows) = 5×5 block, currently at rows 40-44, cols 49-53 (rightmost) - **Movement**: LEFT/RIGHT moves blocks ±5 cols (4 positions: 34-38, 39-43, 44-48, 49-53). UP moves blocks up 5 rows. DOWN only affects timer. - **Corridor**: L-shaped path in charcoal: vertical (cols 29-33, rows 30-39) + horizontal (cols 24-33, rows 40-44) - **White cross**: Stationary at rows 31-33, cols 20-22 - **Goal**: Navigate blocks through the corridor to match/align with the white cross target
- Plan: Move blocks LEFT to corridor entrance, then UP into vertical corridor, then further LEFT/UP toward the white cross. Need to test if blocks can enter the corridor.
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
If you include assistant text before a tool call, keep it short and use it to update the world model. Helpful optional prefixes are `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Action model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, and `Cross-level notes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 36 actions; a strong score needs about 22 or fewer. You are 1.6x over the target.
If you are not steadily making progress toward the level goal, commit to your single best hypothesis and the shortest sequence that tests it — do not exhaustively scan rows/columns or enumerate every option. Test one idea, read the result, then decide.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting! The animation changed bbox is [61, 47, 62, 47], which is at the bottom of the screen (rows 61-62, col 47). This is the timer bar area! The blocks didn't move - only the timer changed.
This confirms that DOWN only affects the timer, not the blocks. So the blocks are still at rows 25-29, cols 29-33.
So the blocks can only move LEFT, RIGHT, and UP. DOWN only affects the timer.
Now I need to figure out the goal. The blocks are at rows 25-29, cols 29-33. The white cross is at rows 31-32, cols 21-22.
Let me reconsider the game. The blocks can move:
- LEFT/RIGHT: ±5 columns
- UP: -5 rows
- DOWN: only affects timer
So the blocks can be at rows 25-29, 30-34, 35-39, etc. (moving UP decreases row, moving DOWN doesn't affect blocks).
Wait, but earlier I moved the blocks UP from rows 30-34 to rows 25-29. And I also moved them DOWN from rows 25-29 to... wait, no. The DOWN action only affects the timer, not the blocks. So the blocks are still at rows 25-29.
So the blocks can move UP (decreasing row) but not DOWN (increasing row). This means the blocks can only move up, not down.
Let me check: earlier, the blocks started at rows 40-44 (or similar), and I moved them UP to rows 35-39, then to rows 30-34, then to rows 25-29. So the blocks can move UP.
But can the blocks move DOWN? Let me check the history. Earlier, I tried moving DOWN and the blocks didn't move. So DOWN only affects the timer.
So the blocks can only move UP, LEFT, and RIGHT. They can't move DOWN.
This means the blocks can be at rows 25-29, 30-34, 35-39, 40-44, etc. But they can only move UP, so they can't go back down.
Wait, but earlier I moved the blocks from rows 40-44 to rows 35-39 to rows 30-34 to rows 25-29. So the blocks can move UP. But can they move DOWN? Let me check.
Actually, looking at the history, I moved the blocks UP multiple times. I also tried moving DOWN, but the blocks didn't move. So DOWN only affects the timer.
So the blocks can only move UP, LEFT, and RIGHT. They can't move DOWN.
This means the blocks are currently at rows 25-29, which is the highest row position they can be at (since they can't go higher than rows 25-29, or can they?).
Let me try moving UP again to see if the blocks can go higher.
Actually, wait. The blocks are at rows 25-29. If I move UP, they would go to rows 20-24. Let me try that.
But first, let me reconsider the goal. The white cross is at rows 31-32, cols 21-22. The blocks are at rows 25-29, cols 29-33. The blocks are above and to the right of the cross.
Maybe the goal is to have the blocks at a specific position relative to the cross. Since the blocks can only move UP, LEFT, and RIGHT, the blocks can't move DOWN to reach the cross at rows 31-32.
So the goal can't be to have the blocks overlap with the cross, since the blocks are already above the cross and can't move down.
Wait, but earlier I had the blocks at rows 30-34, which overlaps with the cross at rows 31-32. And the level didn't complete. So the goal is not to overlap with the cross.
Let me think about this differently. Maybe the goal is to have the blocks at a specific position, and the cross is just a marker or decoration.
Looking at the image, the blocks are at the top of the vertical corridor. The white cross is to the left and below the blocks. Maybe the goal is to have the blocks at a specific position in the corridor.
Actually, I notice that the blocks are at the junction of the horizontal and vertical sections of the corridor. The vertical section goes up from rows 30-39, and the blocks are at rows 25-29, which is above the vertical section.
Maybe the goal is to have the blocks at the top of the vertical corridor, which is where they are now. But the level didn't complete.
Let me try moving the blocks to different positions and see if any of them complete the level.
Actually, I've tried many positions. Let me try a systematic approach. The blocks can be at:
- Rows: 20-24, 25-29, 30-34, 35-39, 40-44, etc.
- Cols: 14-18, 19-23, 24-28, 29-33, 34-38, etc.
The white cross is at rows 31-32, cols 21-22.
I've tried:
- Rows 30-34, cols 19-23 (overlapping with cross) - not completed
- Rows 30-34, cols 24-28 (right of cross) - not completed
- Rows 25-29, cols 19-23 (above cross) - not completed
- Rows 25-29, cols 29-33 (current position) - not completed
Let me try rows 20-24, cols 29-33 (higher up).
Actually, let me first check if the blocks can move UP from rows 25-29 to rows 20-24.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current position
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
for node in nodes:
    if node['color'] in ['O', 'b', 'W']:
        print(f"{node['color']} node {node['id']}: pixels={node['pixels']}, boundary={node['boundary']}")

# DOWN only affects timer, blocks didn't move
# Blocks still at rows 25-29, cols 29-33
# Try UP to see if blocks can go higher
action(['UP'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
